summary: A crystal‑clear soundboard recording of a high‑energy 1992 Chicago show, highlighted by So Many Roads, Lovelight (with James Cotton) and Good Morning Little Schoolgirl.
review: The remastered SBD capture is praised for its pristine clarity, depth and “sonic perfection,” making it a go‑to listen for audiophiles. Musically the concert shines with Jerry’s sparkling solo on So Many Roads, a fiery Lovelight featuring James Cotton, and an inspired second set that includes Good Morning Little Schoolgirl, I KO and a jubilant Gloria encore. While a few reviewers note edited cuts (notably the missing Bertha solo) and occasional mix quirks, the overwhelming response is that both performance and recording are outstanding.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (30+) give 4‑5 stars and extol both the music and sound quality; only a handful criticize edits, so the weighted average of show (≈4.6) and recording (≈4.2) yields ~4.5.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix praised as “sonicly perfect” and ideal for headphones; some users note edited cuts (Bertha solo) and occasional tinny/over‑processed sections.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set sizzles with a seamless flow from IKO into strong jams; guests Steve Miller and James Cotton add excitement, while the encore leaves the audience dancing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s playing is widely praised – sparkling solo on So Many Roads and tight lead work throughout; a few note occasional sleepy moments but overall strong.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mixed remarks – some enjoy his bass foundation, others cite “Phil bombs during Gloria” and prefer being closer to the stage for better sound.
band_member_comments.Bob: Positive vibe, especially his vocal delivery on Good Morning Little Schoolgirl; no major criticisms.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (and earlier Brent) receive both praise (“vastly underrated”) and criticism of patch choices that sounded thin or clunky during this era.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum train‑horn segment is highlighted as a memorable moment; no substantive complaints.
